summary: FDA has granted breakthrough designation to two devices that use generative AI to interpret chest X-rays and draft the radiology reports.

summary: New bill proposes expanding methadone access, $5.7 trillion on U.S. health care, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Facing a brutal run, battered vaccine makers still see cause for hope
summary: It's an unusual time to be in the vaccine business. But in the view of those gathered at the BIO conference this week, it’s not altogether a bleak one.

title: Opinion: My adult daughter has Down syndrome. Her life is entirely different from what experts expected
summary: “Women are fully capable of making difficult decisions, but meaningful choice requires trusted, vetted information,” writes Michelle Sie Whitten of the Global Down Syndrome Foundation.

title: Bipartisan bill seeks to allow direct prescribing of methadone for opioid addiction
summary: Methadone for opioid addiction has been available only at specialty clinics. A bipartisan bill would greatly expand access.

title: STAT+: Biosecure didn’t stop China’s rise in biotech. Some lawmakers want to do more
summary: They're laying the groundwork for legislation and regulation to counter Chinese biopharma companies.

title: Opinion: How a 1976 murder case changed doctor-patient confidentiality
summary: The Tarasoff case remains a pivotal marker in bioethics at the bicentennial as we now reach the country’s 250th birthday.

summary: Eli Lilly is investing in a small startup developing a medication to spur hair growth, and potentially also treat endometriosis.

title: STAT+: U.S. health spending rose sharply in 2025, thanks to GLP-1 use and more care
summary: People are getting more care and using lots of GLP-1 drugs, fueling a sharp increase in health care spending.

title: Judge temporarily blocks subpoenas in criminal probe of transgender care at New York hospitals
summary: A judge temporarily blocked federal prosecutors in Texas from getting access to the medical records of transgender patients treated at New York hospitals on Wednesday.

title: Clinical trial set to test two drugs for fast-growing Ebola outbreak
summary: A clinical trial testing two drugs against the Bundibugyo ebolavirus, which is driving a fast-moving outbreak in Central Africa, is set to begin next week, WHO officials said.

title: Investors pour $115M into RQ Bio in search of a preventive flu drug
summary: The startup claims to have a “differentiated” antibody that can provide long-lasting protection against a variety of influenza strains.

summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: Brittany Trang brings the latest from BIO on how biotech companies are approaching artificial intelligence.

title: STAT+: AI wades into a vexing medical mystery: What causes sudden cardiac death?
summary: A new study published in Nature uses artificial intelligence to identify people at high risk for sudden cardiac death, and pinpoints a possible reason.

title: To Wall Street, psychedelics biotechs are more teammates than rivals
summary: Some analysts argue that, even for competitors, a big clinical trial win for Definium Therapeutics helps validate the psychedelic field and “bring it even further into the mainstream.”

title: STAT+: Wagering on FDA changes, Regenxbio will submit Duchenne gene therapy for approval
summary: Regenxbio plans to submit its Duchenne gene therapy for FDA approval, just a month after it said that regulators wanted to see another trial.

title: Ollin refuels with $330M to back pivotal tests of Vabysmo challenger
summary: One of the year’s largest venture funding rounds comes just after a head-to-head study showed Ollin’s drug — licensed from a China-based developer — had certain advantages over Roche’s eye treatment.

title: Pfizer drug acquired in Seagen deal disappoints in lung cancer study
summary: The setback dims the outlook for a medicine Pfizer sees as an important contributor going forward, though some analysts are optimistic about the drug’s chances in another trial.

title: How Kardigan spun deal leftovers into a $400M IPO
summary: CEO Tassos Gianakakos said having late-stage assets and a team fortified at MyoKardia really resonated with investors. SpaceX's huge public offering didn't hurt either.

title: HHS, responding to China’s rise, moves to fast-track early drug research
summary: A series of newly planned reforms are designed to entice companies to start early trials in the U.S. rather than overseas.

title: Hospitals irate after Eli Lilly follows through on 340B ultimatum
summary: The drugmaker stopped paying 340B discounts to hospitals that didn’t comply with its new paperwork requirements late last week. Hospitals are urging HRSA to step in.

title: Regenxbio to resubmit gene therapy as FDA backtracks on another drug rejection
summary: The agency took another step toward clearing a backlog of Makary-era rejections that appeared to contradict earlier agreements with drugmakers.
